content.subject: maths
content.title: Deba and Deep's Cow Counting Adventure
content.concept: Learning to count using marks.
content.explanation: Deba and Deep learned how to count their cows by making marks on a wall. Each time a cow left, they made a mark, and when they returned, they struck out a mark for each cow that came back. This way, they could easily keep track of their cows! 🐄✏️
examples: If they had 5 cows, they would make 5 marks on the wall.

practice_questions.q: How many cows had reached home if 2 marks were left?
practice_questions.a: They had 2 cows that did not return.

practice_questions.q: Why were Deba and Deep worried?
practice_questions.a: They were worried because they couldn't see all their cows.

content.subject: maths
content.title: What's in a Name?
content.concept: Comparing names by length.
content.explanation: In this lesson, we compare the lengths of different animal names to find out which one is the longest and which one is the shortest. 🐅🐘

practice_questions.q: Who is the captain of the first team?
practice_questions.a: The captain with the longest name.

practice_questions.q: Who has a longer name, Mahesh or Kartik?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Name Counting Exercise
content.concept: Counting and comparing names.
content.explanation: Write down your friends' names and see who has the longest and shortest names! 📋✍️

practice_questions.q: Tick the longest name(s) and cross the shortest name(s).
Count how many names start with the same letter as yours.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Letters in Number Names
content.concept: Identifying numbers with the same number of letters in their names.
content.explanation: Some numbers have names that have the same number of letters. For example, the number 56 is called 'Fifty-six' and has 8 letters! Can you find other numbers between 1 and 99 that also have 8 letters? 🤔

practice_questions.q: Write other numbers between 1 and 99 that have 8 letters.
How many letters does the number name of 17 have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: My Roll Number
content.concept: Writing roll numbers using number cards.
content.explanation: Use number cards to write your roll number! How many letters are in its name? Let's find out! 📝
examples: My roll number is 42 (Forty-two). It has 8 letters.

practice_questions.q: Write your roll number using number cards.
practice_questions.a: My roll number is ____. Its number name has ____ letters.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Find the Number
content.concept: Solving number riddles.
content.explanation: Can you solve these fun number puzzles? They will help you think about numbers in a new way! 🕵️‍♂️

practice_questions.q: My number name has two words. The first word has 6 letters and the second word has 5 letters. I am very near to 100. Who am I?
My friends and I are the numbers from 63 to 78. My number name is the smallest among all my friends. Who am I?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Grouping Household Objects
content.concept: Sorting objects based on characteristics.
content.explanation: Let's group household objects into those that need electricity and those that don't! ⚡️
examples: TV and Lamp need electricity, while Pillow and Chair do not.

practice_questions.q: Write the names of the objects in two groups: Things that need electricity and Things that don't need electricity.
practice_questions.a: Group 1: TV, Lamp; Group 2: Pillow, Chair.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Crafting with Shapes
content.concept: Using shapes to create art.
content.explanation: Jaya is building a rocket using different shapes! What shapes can you use to create your own art? 🚀

practice_questions.q: What shapes did Jaya use to build her rocket?
What shape is between the red cuboid and yellow cuboid?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Faces and Edges
content.concept: Identifying straight and curved edges in shapes.
content.explanation: Let's talk about shapes! Shapes can have straight edges like a square or curved edges like a circle. Can you see which ones are straight and which are curved? 🤔

practice_questions.q: Which shapes have straight edges?
Which shapes have curved edges?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Constructing Houses with Shapes
content.concept: Creating structures using different shapes.
content.explanation: Jaya made some houses using different shapes. Can you build your own houses, towers, or rockets using shapes around you? 🏠🚀
examples: House made of triangles and rectangles.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shapes in Toys
content.concept: Recognizing shapes in everyday objects.
content.explanation: What shapes can you find in your toys? Let's look at Devika's toy engine and count the shapes! 🚂
examples: Red cylinder, yellow cone, grey cuboid.

practice_questions.q: How many red cylinders are there?
What shapes are used in your toys?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shape Construction Game
content.concept: Collaborative learning through shape construction.
content.explanation: In groups, one student selects three shapes and describes how to build them. The others try to recreate it without seeing! 🎲
examples: The cylinder is on top of the cuboid.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shape Hunt
content.concept: Identifying shapes in the environment.
content.explanation: Can you find these shapes in your classroom? Let's fill in the table! 🏫

practice_questions.q: Name the shape you find the most.
Name the shape you find the least.
What objects are made of more than one shape?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Shapes
content.concept: Classifying shapes based on edges and faces.
content.explanation: Let's look at these shapes and answer some questions about their edges and faces! 🧐

practice_questions.q: Name shapes with no edges.
Name shapes with only flat faces.
Name shapes with only curved faces.
Name shapes with both straight and curved edges.
Name shapes with both flat and curved faces.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Clay and Sticks Shapes
content.concept: Creating shapes using clay and sticks.
content.explanation: Look what I made with clay and sticks! Now it's your turn to make shapes too! 🎨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Dice Game
content.concept: Understanding dice and their faces.
content.explanation: Look at a die! What numbers are on the opposite faces? Let's explore! 🎲

practice_questions.q: What number is on the face opposite number 1?
What number is on the face opposite number 2?
What pattern do you notice?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Joining Cubes
content.concept: Exploring combinations of cubes.
content.explanation: In how many different ways can you join 3 cubes? Let's find out! 🧊

practice_questions.q: What shape do you get if you join a cube and a cuboid?
How many cubes are used to make this shape?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Estimating Objects
content.concept: Estimating quantities of objects.
content.explanation: Look at the picture. Can you estimate and write the number of each object? 🍊

practice_questions.q: How many oranges are there?
How many bangles are there?

content.subject: maths
content.title: The Story of Our Numbers
content.concept: Understanding the origin of number systems.
content.explanation: Long ago, people began counting to keep track of their belongings. They used marks on cave walls and tree barks. Ancient Indians invented a system using ten symbols (0-9) to write any number, which is still used today! 🌍✨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Snakes and Ladders Questions
content.concept: Applying number concepts through a game.
content.explanation: Answer the following questions based on the Snakes and Ladders board. 🐍🪜

practice_questions.q: Which number will you reach if you take the ladder from 13?
If you are on the snake at number 25, which number will you reach?
You are standing on 96. Which number on the die will take you to the snake's mouth?
Show the number written on the tail of the longest snake using bundles and loose sticks.

content.subject: maths
content.title: The Talking Pot
content.concept: Learning numbers through a fun rhyme.
content.explanation: Join the talking pot as it helps you find the next number! 🎶

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.concept: Finding pairs of numbers that add up to 100.
content.explanation: Bholu made 100 by jumping on 65 and jumped 35 more. Can you find other pairs? 🤔

practice_questions.q: Fill in the blanks: 60 and 40 makes ..............
Fill in the blanks: 45 and ............. makes 100.
Fill in the blanks: ............. and 25 makes 100.
Fill in the blanks: ............. and ............. makes 100.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Estimating Matchsticks
content.concept: Estimating and counting quantities.
content.explanation: Let's estimate and count matchsticks! How close can you get to 100? 📦✨

practice_questions.q: Estimate the number of matchsticks in the box:
Count the number of matchsticks in the box:
How many boxes of matchsticks will get the total close to 100 matchsticks?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Numbers Beyond 100
content.concept: Learning to write numbers beyond 100.
content.explanation: When we have 100 and we add more, we can create new numbers! For example, 100 and 1 makes 101. Let's fill in the blanks to see how numbers grow! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is 100 and 3?
What is 100 and 5?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Complete the Number Table
content.concept: Filling in missing numbers based on patterns.
content.explanation: Let's see how many numbers we can fill in! Look at the pattern and write the missing numbers. ✏️

practice_questions.q: 100 and 4 makes One Hundred ___?
100 and 6 makes One Hundred ___?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guess the Number Game
content.concept: Using actions to represent numbers.
content.explanation: In this fun game, one team will show a number using claps, snaps, and pats, while the other team guesses! 🎶
examples: Clap – Snap Snap – Pat means 123 (One hundred and twenty three)

content.subject: maths
content.title: Estimate and Count Seeds
content.concept: Estimating quantities and counting accurately.
content.explanation: Let's fill a container with seeds and guess how many there are! Then, we will count to see how close we were. 🌱

practice_questions.q: How many seeds do you think are in the container?
How many seeds did you actually count?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding the Number Line
content.concept: Using a number line to visualize numbers.
content.explanation: A number line helps us see where numbers are placed. Let's practice placing numbers on the line! 📏

content.subject: maths
content.title: Jumping Game
content.concept: Understanding number line and jumps.
content.explanation: Let's learn how to jump on a number line! 🎉 We can fill in the missing numbers by jumping forward or backward.
examples: Jump back from 160 to 140 by subtracting 20! ➖

practice_questions.q: What number comes after 120?
What number comes before 140?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Representing Numbers
content.concept: Different ways to represent numbers.
content.explanation: We can represent numbers in different forms! Let's see how we can use matchstick bundles and number sentences. 🧮
examples: 125 can be made with 1 bundle of 100 and 25 single sticks.
145 can be made with 1 bundle of 100, 4 bundles of 10, and 5 single sticks.

practice_questions.q: How can you represent 172?
practice_questions.a: 1 bundle of 100, 7 bundles of 10, and 2 single sticks.

practice_questions.q: What is 30 more than 150?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Chirag and Nandini's Adventure
content.concept: Using subtraction in a fun way.
content.explanation: Chirag and Nandini love their Nani Maa! She shows them a magic trick with hidden seeds. 🌱 Can you guess how many seeds are hidden?
examples: If there are 15 seeds in total and 12 are on the table, how many are hidden? 🤔

practice_questions.q: If you have 10 seeds and 4 are on the table, how many are hidden?
If there are 20 seeds and 15 are on the table, how many are hidden?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Balushahi Treats
content.concept: Adding and subtracting quantities.
content.explanation: Nandini and Chirag have some delicious balushahi! 🍩 Let's figure out how many they have together and how many they ate.

practice_questions.q: If Chirag eats 2 balushahi, how many are left?
How many total balushahi do they have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Books
content.concept: Finding total quantities.
content.explanation: Chirag and Nandini brought books for Nani Maa! 📚 Let's count how many books they have together.

practice_questions.q: If Nandini has 7 books and Chirag has 9, how many do they have in total?
If they buy 4 more books, how many do they have now?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Number Cards
content.concept: Playing a game to reinforce addition and subtraction.
content.explanation: Let's play a card game! 🎴 You can collect cards by making addition or subtraction statements. The more you collect, the better!
examples: If you have 2, 4, and 6, you can keep them because 2 + 4 = 6!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Stamps
content.concept: Adding and subtracting stamps.
content.explanation: Nandini and Chirag have some stamps. Let's find out how many they have together! If they have 22 stamps and get 30 more, how many do they have now? 🤔🍎

practice_questions.q: How many stamps do they have now if they have 22 stamps and get 30 more?
If they have 37 stamps with famous persons, how many stamps do they have with monuments?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using the Number Line
content.concept: Solving problems using a number line.
content.explanation: We can jump on a number line to solve addition and subtraction problems! 🎉 Let's see how many more stamps they need to reach 75. 🐸
examples: Jumping from 52 to 75 means they need 23 more stamps.

practice_questions.q: How many more stamps will they need to reach 75?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Frog and Grasshopper Jumps
content.concept: Understanding jumps on a number line.
content.explanation: A frog jumps forward by 10 each time, while a grasshopper jumps backward by 10. Let's mark where they land! 🐸🐞
examples: If the frog starts at 7, it will land on 17, 27, 37, etc.

practice_questions.q: If the frog is at 7 and jumps 10, where will it land?
If the grasshopper is at 20 and jumps backward by 10, where will it land?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Adding and Subtracting with Dice
content.concept: Playing a grid game to practice addition and subtraction.
content.explanation: Let's play a fun grid game! Roll two dice to make a two-digit number and move your counter. The first to reach between 91 and 100 wins! 🎲🏆
examples: If you roll a 5 and a 3, you can move to 53 or 35.

practice_questions.q: What happens if you can't move forward?
practice_questions.a: You can move backward or roll again!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Jumping on the Number Grid
content.concept: Using a number grid to solve problems.
content.explanation: Let's use the number grid to answer some questions! Draw arrows to show your jumps! 📏✏️
examples: If Nandini is at 45 and gets 34, she will land on 79.

practice_questions.q: Nandini is at 45. She gets 34. Where will she land?
Chirag is at 75. He gets 56. Where will he land?
practice_questions.a: 131 (but we can only go to 100, so he wins!)

content.subject: maths
content.title: Moving with Dice
content.concept: Using dice to move on a number line.
content.explanation: When you roll a dice, you can move forward by the number shown. For example, if you roll an 11, you can move from 19 to 30! 🎲➡️
examples: If you start at 19 and roll a 54, where do you land?
If you roll a 2 from 34, where will you go?

practice_questions.q: If you start at 25 and roll a 10, where do you land?
Chirag rolls a 5 from 34. Where does he go?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Magic Squares
content.concept: Understanding magic squares and their sums.
content.explanation: In a magic square, the numbers in each row, column, and diagonal add up to the same number! 🌟✨
examples: In this square: 2, 7, 6, 9, 5, 1, 4, 3, 8 all add up to 15.

practice_questions.q: What is the magic sum for the numbers in this square?
Can you create your own magic square?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Nandini and Chirag's Puzzles
content.concept: Exploring numbers through puzzles.
content.explanation: Nandini and Chirag discover puzzles that Nani Maa solved, which helps them learn about numbers! 🧩📚

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Radishes
content.concept: Adding quantities to find totals.
content.explanation: Nani Maa plucked 25 red radishes and 36 white radishes. How many radishes did she pluck in total? Let's add! 🍅🌱

practice_questions.q: How many radishes in total?
Estimate the total number of radishes.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Magic Magic Magic!
content.concept: Fun with adding numbers in a magic square.
content.explanation: This rhyme helps you remember how to find the magic sums in a square! 🎶✨
content.text:
Magic magic magic!!!
With numbers 1 to 9
Add the numbers in a line
From left to right
And from top to bottom
Did you find the magic?
Now, try from right to left
And from bottom to top
Isn’t it magical?
There is something more,
something more
Add the numbers on the diagonal
Isn’t it magical?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Books Sold by Babli Didi
content.concept: Adding and subtracting numbers to find totals.
content.explanation: Let's find out how many books Babli sold in total! 📚 If she sold 34 books on Monday and 45 books on Tuesday, we can add these two numbers together to find the total. We can also see how many more she sold on Tuesday than Monday by subtracting.

practice_questions.q: Babli didi sold 34 books on Monday and 45 books on Tuesday. How many books did she sell in total?
How many more books did she sell on Tuesday than on Monday?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Cricket Match Runs
content.concept: Finding total runs scored by adding two numbers.
content.explanation: In a cricket match, Team Red scored 56 runs before lunch and 65 runs after lunch. Let's add these two scores to find out how many runs they made in total! 🏏

practice_questions.q: Team Red made 56 runs before lunch and 65 runs after lunch. How many total runs did they make?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Vadas Sold by Rama
content.concept: Adding numbers to find total sales.
content.explanation: Rama sells vadas in the school canteen. If she sold 39 vadas on the first day and 12 more the next day, we can find out how many she sold in total by adding these two numbers! 🍽️

practice_questions.q: Rama sold 39 vadas the first day and 12 more the next day. How many vadas did she sell in total?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Plants Dried Up
content.concept: Finding the difference between two numbers.
content.explanation: Gehu brought 56 plants for her garden, but some dried up. If she is left with 29 plants, we can find out how many dried up by subtracting! 🌱

practice_questions.q: Gehu brought 56 plants and is left with 29. How many plants dried up?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Create a Word Problem
content.concept: Using numbers to create a word problem.
content.explanation: Choose two numbers and make your own word problem! Share it with your classmates. This helps you think creatively and practice math! ✏️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Reach 100 Game
content.concept: Adding numbers in turns to reach a target.
content.explanation: This game is played between two children. The first player says a number between 1 and 10, and the second player adds a number between 1 and 10 to it. Keep adding until someone reaches 100! 🎉
examples: Player 1 starts with 5, Player 2 adds 7, Total = 12.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Estimating Sums
content.concept: Estimating answers to addition problems.
content.explanation: Estimate the answer and say if it will be more or less than 100. For example, 150 - 50 is 100, so 150 - 49 is more than 100! 🧮

practice_questions.q: Is 134 - 56 more or less than 100?
Is 199 - 90 more or less than 100?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using a Number Line
content.concept: Visualizing addition and subtraction using a number line.
content.explanation: You can use a number line to solve problems! Start at any number and jump to find the answer. You can even use open number lines! 📏
examples: Starting at 0, jump to 10, then to 20 to find 20.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fun with Shapes
content.concept: Identifying and creating shapes.
content.explanation: Let's explore shapes! You can make a rangoli using different shapes. What shapes do you see? 🌼

practice_questions.q: Name the shapes drawn in Amma’s rangoli.
How many shapes are made with curved lines?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Your Own Rangoli
content.concept: Using cutouts to create designs.
content.explanation: Use cutouts of shapes to make a rangoli design! Outline the object and color it. Be creative! 🎨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Rectangles
content.concept: Rectangles have opposite sides that are equal.
content.explanation: A rectangle is a shape with four sides where the opposite sides are equal in length. For example, if you have a matchbox, it usually has a rectangular shape! 📦

practice_questions.q: Name any three objects that have rectangular faces?
practice_questions.a: A book, a table, and a smartphone.

practice_questions.q: How many different faces did you get?
What shapes are these faces?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Squares
content.concept: A square has all sides equal.
content.explanation: A square is a special type of rectangle where all four sides are the same length. For example, a chessboard is made up of squares! ♟️

practice_questions.q: How many squares do you see in this drawing?
practice_questions.a: Count them and write the number.

practice_questions.q: How many corners does a square have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Tracing Shapes
content.concept: Tracing helps us understand shapes better.
content.explanation: You can trace the shapes of different objects to see how they look! Try tracing a cuboid or a square. ✏️

practice_questions.q: Trace all the faces of any cuboidal object.
practice_questions.a: Draw the outlines of the object.

practice_questions.q: Can a cuboid have a face like a triangle?
practice_questions.a: No, a cuboid has rectangular faces.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Same to Same
content.concept: Understanding the difference between squares and rectangles.
content.explanation: This rhyme helps you remember the key differences between squares and rectangles!
content.text:
My aunt gifted us a board game.
I wonder what is the difference between a square and a rectangle?
I found a difference. Look at their sides.
In a rectangle, all sides do not necessarily have to be equal in length.
It is enough to have just the opposite sides be equal in length.
Oh! These tiles are exactly like squares!
The chess and ludo boards are also square in shape.
In a square, all sides must be equal in length.
But, when I fold them, they become a rectangle.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Building Shapes
content.concept: Creating shapes with sticks.
content.explanation: Use matchsticks to create different shapes and see how many you can make! 🏗️

practice_questions.q: Use matchsticks to make a square so that it has squares on all its sides. How many squares did you get?
Can you fold all the corners of a square sheet in such a way that the number of corners remains the same?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Square Corners
content.concept: Identifying square corners in shapes.
content.explanation: A square corner is where two lines meet to form a right angle (90 degrees). You can check corners by stacking squares or rectangles! 📐

practice_questions.q: Are the corners of a square the same?
practice_questions.a: Yes, all corners of a square are square corners.

practice_questions.q: How do you know?
practice_questions.a: You can pile up squares to see!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Marking Square Corners
content.concept: Practicing identifying square corners.
content.explanation: Let's find and mark square corners in different shapes! ✏️

practice_questions.q: Mark the square corners in these shapes.
practice_questions.a: Draw a dot at each square corner.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Triangles
content.concept: Identifying and describing triangles.
content.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners! Let's find triangles around us! 🔺

practice_questions.q: How many different triangles can be made using the dots on this circle?
practice_questions.a: Count the triangles you can form!

practice_questions.q: What do all these triangular objects have in common?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Triangles
content.concept: Building triangles with matchsticks.
content.explanation: Use matchsticks to create triangles! You can even move them to make new shapes! 🌟

practice_questions.q: Move two matchsticks to turn one triangle into two triangles.
practice_questions.a: Try it and see how many triangles you can make!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Circles
content.concept: Identifying circles and their properties.
content.explanation: A circle is round and has no corners! Let's find circular objects around us! 🔵

practice_questions.q: What does a circle look like?
practice_questions.a: A round shape with no corners.

practice_questions.q: Name some objects that are like circles.
practice_questions.a: A wheel, a coin, and a plate!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Drawing Circles
content.concept: Practicing drawing circles.
content.explanation: Let's draw circles using different objects like bottle caps and bangles! 🎨

practice_questions.q: Draw colourful circles to complete the circus scene.
practice_questions.a: Use your creativity to make it fun!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Shapes
content.concept: Identifying and comparing shapes.
content.explanation: Let's explore different shapes like squares and triangles! 🟦🔺 Both have straight edges but differ in the number of corners and sides.

practice_questions.q: How many corners does a triangle have?
What shape has 4 sides?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Find the Largest Rectangle
content.concept: Identifying the largest rectangle among shapes.
content.explanation: Look at the shapes and find the biggest rectangle! 🟩

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Triangles
content.concept: Counting the number of triangles in a figure.
content.explanation: Count how many triangles are in the second figure! 🔺🔺🔺

practice_questions.q: How many triangles are there in the second figure?
If I make one more row, how many triangles will be there?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Completing Rectangles
content.concept: Filling gaps to complete a rectangle.
content.explanation: Tick the appropriate shapes to fill the gaps and complete the rectangle! 🟩✅

content.subject: maths
content.title: Splitting Shapes
content.concept: Dividing shapes into triangles.
content.explanation: Draw lines to split the shape into triangles! ✏️🔺

practice_questions.q: Draw two lines to split the shape into three triangles.
Draw one line to split the shape into 3 triangles.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Shapes with Tangram
content.concept: Using tangram pieces to create shapes.
content.explanation: Can you create these shapes using tangram pieces? 🧩✨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Ajji, Teji and Jojo at the Mela
content.concept: Counting objects at the mela.
content.explanation: Ajji, Teji, and Jojo are at the mela counting triangular torans! How many can you guess? 🎉🔺

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Bangles and Toffees
content.concept: Counting and comparing quantities.
content.explanation: Guess how many bangles and toffees there are! Can you count them? 🎊🍬

practice_questions.q: How many bangles are there?
How many toffees does Jojo have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill in the Number Tiles
content.concept: Completing number sequences.
content.explanation: Fill in the empty tiles with numbers! 🟦🔢

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guessing the Number of Ants
content.concept: Estimating and counting objects.
content.explanation: How many ants do you think there are? Count and check! 🐜🔍

content.subject: maths
content.title: Writing Numbers with Matchsticks
content.concept: Learn to write numbers using matchstick bundles.
content.explanation: Teji and Jojo use matchsticks to represent numbers! For example, 235 can be shown as 200 and 35 more (200 + 35) or as 15 less than 250 (250 - 15). 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Placing Numbers on the Number Line
content.concept: Understanding where numbers lie on a number line.
content.explanation: Place the number 235 on the number line between 200 and 250. Can you see how it fits? 🏷️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Increasing and Decreasing Numbers
content.concept: Practice increasing and decreasing numbers.
content.explanation: Look at the pictures and write the corresponding numbers! For example, if you have 285, increase it by one to get 286! 📈

practice_questions.q: What is 147 increased by ten?
What is 289 decreased by 10?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Flag Game
content.concept: Play a guessing game with numbers.
content.explanation: Teji thinks of a number between 200 and 210. Jojo has to guess it! If the flag points right, the number is higher; if it points left, it's lower! 🎈

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Letters in Number Names
content.concept: Count letters in number names and see the pattern.
content.explanation: Write down a number name, count the letters, and write the new number name! What happens? For example, 'Two hundred and thirty-five' has 11 letters! ✍️
examples: Two hundred and thirty-five ➔ 11 letters ➔ Twenty-three

content.subject: maths
content.title: Locating Numbers on the Number Line
content.concept: Locate specific numbers on a number line.
content.explanation: Find 216, 243, and 257 on the number line! How far is 387 from 400? Let's jump to find out! 🏃‍♂️

practice_questions.q: How far is 387 from 400?
Which is more: 393 or 400?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Sweets at the Shop
content.concept: Solve problems related to counting sweets.
content.explanation: Teji and Jojo visit Farooq Chacha’s Sweet Shop! How many pieces of Mysore pak are there? Let's count together! 🍬

practice_questions.q: How many pieces of Mysore pak are in one tray?
How many laddoos does Chacha have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding House Numbers
content.concept: Identifying and filling in house numbers based on patterns.
content.explanation: Let's find out how many houses are there and their numbers! 🏠 Count the shaded houses and fill in the numbers by looking for patterns. For example, if 105 is one house, what is the next one? 🤔
examples: If 105 is a house number, then 106 is the next one! 🎉
If 113 is 10 floors above 13, what is the number of 13?

practice_questions.q: What is one floor above 201?
What do you notice about the house numbers when moving up and down?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Arvind Dada's Sweet Delivery
content.concept: Coloring and identifying house numbers for delivery.
content.explanation: Arvind Dada has to deliver sweets! 🍬 Color the houses where he needs to deliver sweets based on the house numbers provided.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Locate the House Numbers
content.concept: Finding and writing house numbers based on given clues.
content.explanation: Can you find the house numbers from the building? 🏢 Write down the floor and column for each house number!
examples: House number 131 is on the 1st floor, 3rd column.

practice_questions.q: What is the floor and column for house number 106?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guess the Number
content.concept: Riddles based on number properties.
content.explanation: Can you guess the number based on the clues? 🤔 Let's solve these fun riddles!
examples: I have digits 9, 1, and 5. I am less than 200. Which number am I?

practice_questions.q: I am greater than 300 but less than 400. I have no tens. My ones and hundreds digits are the same. Which number am I?
I am a 3-digit number. I have only digits 4 and 0. Which number am I?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Arvind Dada's Packing Challenge
content.concept: Understanding the relationship between boxes of sweets.
content.explanation: How many boxes of 10 can fit in a box of 100? 📦 Let's find out! Fill in the blanks with your answers.
examples: 1 box of 100 can fit 10 boxes of 10.

practice_questions.q: How many boxes of 10 can he fit in two boxes of 100?
How many boxes of 10 can he fit in four boxes of 100?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Find the Numbers!
content.concept: Identifying numbers with specific digits.
content.explanation: Let's go on a number hunt! 🕵️‍♂️ Write the numbers between 200 and 300 that have 5 as a digit.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Greater and Lesser
content.concept: Learning to compare numbers using > and < signs.
content.explanation: When we compare numbers, we look at their values. The bigger number is greater (>) and the smaller number is lesser (<). For example, 487 is greater than 423 because it has more laddoos! 🍬

practice_questions.q: Is 199 greater than or less than 201?
Is 285 greater than or less than 275?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Comparison Exercises
content.concept: Practice comparing numbers.
content.explanation: Let's practice comparing numbers! Use the signs > and < to show which number is bigger or smaller.

practice_questions.q: Compare 329 and 392.
Compare 110 and 11.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Identifying Smallest and Greatest
content.concept: Finding the smallest and greatest numbers in a set.
content.explanation: Let's find the smallest and greatest numbers in each row! 🌟

practice_questions.q: Circle the smallest number: 374, 473, 347, 437
Circle the greatest number: 466, 437, 439, 447, 483

content.subject: maths
content.title: Making 3-Digit Numbers
content.concept: Forming 3-digit numbers with given digits.
content.explanation: Let's create 3-digit numbers using the digits 3, 2, and 4! Remember, no repeating digits! 🎉

practice_questions.q: Make a 3-digit number using 3, 2, and 4.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Gopal's Festival
content.concept: Understanding the excitement of festivals.
content.explanation: Gopal and Dhara are excited for their favorite festival! They clean and decorate their house for the special day. 🎊

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Rakhis for Raksha Bandhan
content.concept: Learning to make Rakhis for the festival.
content.explanation: Let's make Rakhis! For each Rakhi, we need one flower, some threads, and beads. How many do we need for 5 Rakhis? 🌸

practice_questions.q: If we need 1 flower, 2 threads, and 3 beads for each Rakhi, how many do we need for 5 Rakhis?
practice_questions.a: We need 5 flowers, 10 threads, and 15 beads.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Materials Needed for Rakhis
content.concept: Understanding multiplication through real-life examples.
content.explanation: To make Rakhis for Raksha Bandhan, we need to calculate how many flowers, threads, and beads are required. Let's see how we can do this! 🌸🧵💎

practice_questions.q: For making 10 Rakhis, how many flowers do we need?
If we have 30 flowers, how many Rakhis can we make?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Laddoos in Boxes
content.concept: Using addition and multiplication to count items.
content.explanation: When we have boxes of laddoos, we can count them using addition or multiplication! 🍬

practice_questions.q: If there are 18 laddoos and 9 people, how many laddoos does each person get?
How many laddoos are there if you count 6 times 3?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Sharing Kaju Katlis Equally
content.concept: Understanding division through sharing.
content.explanation: When we have kaju katlis, we can share them equally among friends! 🍬🤗
examples: 20 kaju katlis shared by 5 people gives each person 4 kaju katlis (20 ÷ 5 = 4) 🍬
16 kaju katlis shared by 4 people gives each person 4 kaju katlis (16 ÷ 4 = 4) 🍬

practice_questions.q: If you have 15 pedas and share them among 5 friends, how many does each get?
How many cycles can be fitted with 12 wheels if each cycle needs 2 wheels?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Jalebis in Groups
content.concept: Counting items in groups to make it easier.
content.explanation: When counting jalebis, we can group them to make counting easier! 🍬

practice_questions.q: How many jalebis are there if you count 6 groups of 4?
If you have 24 jalebis and want to share with 6 friends, how many does each get?

content.subject: maths
content.title: How many jalebis to buy?
content.concept: Division and multiplication in sharing.
content.explanation: If there are 9 family members and each wants 4 jalebis, we can find out how many jalebis we need by multiplying! 🍬🍬🍬

practice_questions.q: How many jalebis should Dhara buy if she has 24?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Different ways of grouping plants
content.concept: Understanding multiplication through grouping.
content.explanation: Dhara and Gopal are figuring out how to group plants! 🌼🌼🌼 They can group them in different ways.

practice_questions.q: Can you find more equal groups of different sizes?
practice_questions.a: Draw them and write as multiplication!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting chickens at the farm
content.concept: Using multiplication to count.
content.explanation: Let's count the chickens! 🐔🐔🐔 We can use multiplication to make it easier.

practice_questions.q: What is 4 times 2?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Skip Jumping by 3
content.concept: Understanding skip counting.
content.explanation: Dhara is skip jumping! 🏃‍♀️✨ She jumps by 3 each time. Let's see where she lands!

practice_questions.q: What number will Dhara jump onto after 9?
Is there a pattern in these numbers: 3, 6, 9, ...?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Continue Skip Jumping
content.concept: Practicing skip counting.
content.explanation: Let's continue skip jumping by 6! 🏃‍♀️💨

practice_questions.q: Continue skip jumping by 6 starting from 0.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Constructing Times Tables
content.concept: Creating multiplication tables.
content.explanation: Let's create the times-6 table using skip jumps! 📊

practice_questions.q: What times table can you construct from Gopal’s jumps?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Constructing Times Tables
content.concept: Learning to construct multiplication tables using skip counting.
content.explanation: Let's learn how to create multiplication tables by skipping numbers! For example, if we want to make the 5 times table, we can skip count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, and so on! 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Skip Jumping to Reach the Flower
content.concept: Using skip counting to reach a target number.
content.explanation: In this fun game, you need to skip jump to reach a flower placed on the number 12! You can choose a skip jumping number and see how many jumps it takes you to get there! 🌼

practice_questions.q: What skip jumping number will you choose?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Seeing Patterns in Multiplication Tables
content.concept: Identifying patterns in multiplication results.
content.explanation: When we look at the 5 times table, we can see that the last digits repeat every 10 numbers! Can you guess what the last digit of 11 × 5 will be? 🤔

practice_questions.q: Give 3 examples of numbers that when taken 5 times gives an answer ending with 0.
Give 3 examples of numbers that when taken 5 times gives an answer ending with 5.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Draw and Solve
content.concept: Using drawings to solve multiplication problems.
content.explanation: Let's draw pictures to help us solve multiplication problems! For example, if you have 5 jars with 4 cookies each, you can draw the jars and count the cookies! 🍪

practice_questions.q: There are 5 jars with 4 cookies in each jar. How many cookies are there?
An idli vessel contains 6 idli plates. In each plate we can make 4 idlis. How many idlis can we make?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Distributing Cookies
content.concept: Dividing cookies equally among children.
content.explanation: If you have 30 cookies and want to share them with 5 friends, how many cookies does each friend get? 🍪👦👧

practice_questions.q: How many cookies will each child get if 30 cookies are distributed among 5 children?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Roro's Jumps
content.concept: Finding the size of Roro's jumps.
content.explanation: Roro jumps from 0 to 18 in 6 jumps. To find the size of each jump, we divide the total distance by the number of jumps. 🐸➡️18

practice_questions.q: What is the size of Roro’s jump?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Suma's Savings
content.concept: Calculating days to save a certain amount.
content.explanation: Suma saves ₹8 every day. If she wants to save ₹56, how many days will it take? 💰📅

practice_questions.q: After how many days will Suma have ₹56?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Mary's Sea Shells
content.concept: Finding remaining items after distribution.
content.explanation: Mary has 63 sea shells and gives 7 to each of her 5 friends. Let's find out how many she has left! 🐚👩‍👧‍👦

practice_questions.q: How many sea shells does Mary have left?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Solve the Problems
content.concept: Practice basic multiplication and division.
content.explanation: Let's solve these problems together! 🤔✏️

practice_questions.q: What is 4 × 9?
What is 32 ÷ 8?
What is 6 × 7?
What is 45 ÷ 5?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Multiplication Patterns
content.concept: Understanding multiplication patterns.
content.explanation: Let's explore how numbers multiply and find patterns! 🔍✨

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Spokes
content.concept: Calculating total spokes needed for wheels.
content.explanation: Let's help Bhim calculate how many spokes he needs for his wheels! 🚲🔧

practice_questions.q: How many spokes are needed for 10 wheels if each wheel needs 5 spokes?
How many spokes are needed for 30 wheels?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Spider Legs
content.concept: Calculating total legs of spiders.
content.explanation: If one spider has 8 legs, how many legs do 5 spiders have? 🕷️🦵

practice_questions.q: How many legs will 23 spiders have?
A group of spiders has 32 legs. How many spiders are there?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Wheels
content.concept: Calculating total wheels in auto rickshaws.
content.explanation: How many wheels are there in multiple auto rickshaws? 🚖🔄

practice_questions.q: How many wheels are there in 18 auto rickshaws?
How many wheels are there in 34 auto rickshaws?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Frog's Backward Jumps
content.concept: Understanding backward counting with jumps.
content.explanation: If a frog jumps backwards by 7, what numbers can it reach? 🐸🔙

practice_questions.q: What is the largest number the frog can reach before crossing 50?
What numbers should the frog start from to reach 0?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Ants and Cows
content.concept: Calculating total legs of ants and cows.
content.explanation: Let's find out how many legs are there in total! 🐜🐄

practice_questions.q: If there are 55 ants, how many legs do they have?
If Micky sees 48 legs of cows, how many cows are there?
If Karry sees 24 horns of cows, how many legs are there?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Cost of Wall Hangings
content.concept: Calculating total cost of items.
content.explanation: If one wall-hanging costs ₹42, how much do two cost? 🖼️💵

practice_questions.q: How much do two wall hangings cost?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Money Exchange
content.concept: How to calculate total cost and change during a transaction.
content.explanation: Preeti buys 5 cups of Rabdi and pays with a ₹100 note. Let's find out how much she needs to pay and how much change she will get! 🥛💵
examples: If each cup costs ₹20, then 5 cups cost ₹100. Preeti gives ₹100 and gets ₹0 back.

practice_questions.q: How many ₹100 notes should Preeti give the shopkeeper?
What is the total cost of 5 cups of Rabdi?
How much will the shopkeeper return to Preeti?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Dhruv's Sea Shell Adventure
content.concept: Counting and sharing sea shells.
content.explanation: Dhruv collected 112 sea shells and wants to make necklaces for his friends. Let's see if he has enough! 🐚✨
examples: If Dhruv takes 28 shells for one necklace, how many shells will he have left after making three necklaces?

practice_questions.q: How many necklaces can Dhruv make from 112 shells?
How many shells are left after making 3 necklaces of 28 shells each?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Making Necklaces with Sea Shells
content.concept: Division and sharing in real-life scenarios.
content.explanation: Let's help Dhruv figure out how many necklaces he can make and how many shells he has left! 🐚💍
examples: If Dhruv uses 28 shells for each necklace, he can make 4 necklaces from 112 shells.

practice_questions.q: Kannu makes a necklace of 17 sea-shells. How many such necklaces can be made using 100 sea-shells?
Dhruv finds 127 shiny pebbles and distributes them equally to 3 friends. How many will each get?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Sharing Equally
content.concept: Understanding how to share objects equally.
content.explanation: Shabnam and Mukta are sharing a paratha and chocolates. Let's learn how to share things equally! 🍫🍽️
examples: If a paratha is shared between 2 people, each gets half!

practice_questions.q: How do you share objects equally?
practice_questions.a: By dividing them into equal parts.

practice_questions.q: How many halves make one whole?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Activities for Fair Sharing
content.concept: Practical exercises on sharing equally.
content.explanation: Let's practice sharing with fun activities! 🎉✂️
examples: Draw lines to show different ways of making a half.

practice_questions.q: Tick the paratha that has been cut equally.
practice_questions.a: Choose the one that looks equal.

practice_questions.q: Draw a line to show one-half of the whole.
practice_questions.a: Draw a line down the middle.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Halves and Quarters
content.concept: Learning how to share equally using fractions.
content.explanation: When we share things equally, we can use fractions! For example, if you have 8 marbles and you share them with a friend, each of you gets 4 marbles. That's half! 🍬
examples: If you have 6 apples and share with 2 friends, each gets 2 apples (half). 🍏
When sharing a pizza with 4 friends, each person gets 1/4 of the pizza. 🍕

practice_questions.q: Is this a fair way of sharing? Is there another way to share it fairly?
What part of the chikki did each get?
practice_questions.a: Each person gets a quarter of the chikki.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill in the Blanks
content.concept: Understanding the relationship between halves and doubles.
content.explanation: Let's see how many marbles we can double or halve! If I have 4 marbles, how many is that of 8? 🤔

practice_questions.q: Fill in the blanks: 4 marbles are ______ of 8 marbles.
How far is 5 from half of 14?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fraction Riddles
content.concept: Using clues to identify fractions.
content.explanation: Can you guess the fraction based on the clues? 🕵️‍♂️
examples: I have less than double of 3 marbles. I have more than half of 8 marbles.

practice_questions.q: I have less than double of 4 marbles. I have more than half of 10 marbles. What fraction am I?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Quarters
content.concept: Learning about quarters and how to visualize them.
content.explanation: When we divide something into 4 equal parts, each part is called a quarter! 🍰
examples: When a whole pizza is shared among 4 people, each gets 1/4 of the pizza.

practice_questions.q: How many quarters in a whole?
practice_questions.a: There are 4 quarters in a whole.

practice_questions.q: Tick the objects that show quarters.
practice_questions.a: Look for shapes divided into 4 equal parts!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Choose the Right Fraction
content.concept: Identifying whether to use half or quarter.
content.explanation: Let's decide if we should say half or a quarter! 🤔
examples: Shabnam has coloured half of the birds. Mukta has coloured a quarter.

practice_questions.q: Shabnam has coloured half/a quarter of the birds.
Lakshanya tied half/a quarter of her flowers.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Counting Crows with Akbar and Birbal
content.concept: Estimating quantities through counting.
content.explanation: Akbar wanted to know how many crows were in the city. Birbal estimated there were 963 crows! 🐦 How do you think he figured that out? Sometimes, we can guess how many things there are by thinking about how they might behave.
examples: If I see 5 apples 🍏 on a table, I can guess there might be more in the basket! 🍏🍏🍏🍏🍏

practice_questions.q: How many crows did Birbal say there were?
What did Akbar offer for the correct answer?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Numbers with Tiles
content.concept: Representing numbers using tiles.
content.explanation: You can draw tiles to represent numbers! For example, to show the number 832, you can draw 8 hundreds, 3 tens, and 2 ones. 🎨 Let's practice this with different numbers!

practice_questions.q: How would you represent the number 726?
What does the number 504 represent?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Locating Numbers on the Number Line
content.concept: Finding numbers on a number line.
content.explanation: Let's locate numbers on a number line! 📏 It's like a treasure map where each number has its special place. Can you find where 530 and 628 are?

practice_questions.q: Where is 590 located on the number line?
Locate 759 on the number line.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Playing Tambola with Numbers
content.concept: Using clues to identify numbers.
content.explanation: Let's play a fun game of Tambola! 🎉 You will fill a grid with numbers and strike out the ones that match the clues. The one with the most strikes wins! 🏆
examples: If the clue is 'A number with 4', you can strike out 574! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What number is between 595 and 605?
What is two more than 610?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Helping the Crane Reach Its Food
content.concept: Understanding number patterns and jumps.
content.explanation: Teji and Jojo are helping a crane reach its food! 🦢 Let's help them figure out how many steps the crane needs to take. Can you find the right jumps?
examples: To reach the worm, the crane needs to jump from 667 to 700!

practice_questions.q: How many steps does the crane need to reach 650 from 667?
What is the jump from 750 to 800?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Number Sentences
content.concept: Creating number sentences with given numbers.
content.explanation: Let's write number sentences using the numbers in the center! For example, if we have 290 and 110, we can write 290 + 110 = 400! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is 290 + 110?
What is 750 + 25?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Matching Quantities
content.concept: Matching numbers with quantities.
content.explanation: Can you match the number of children in your school with the right number range? Let's see how many books are in your classroom! 📚

practice_questions.q: Match the number of stars in the sky with a range.
How many pages are in your mathematics book?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Special Numbers
content.concept: Identifying special numbers and patterns.
content.explanation: Let's have some fun with numbers! Can you find all the hundreds? 🎈
content.content:
Let us have some fun, with numbers and patterns, everyone!
Look at the hundreds – 100, 200, 300
Can you find all the hundreds?
Some numbers are special, let's explore,
789, 345, and 123, what more?
Jojo wonders why they're so neat,
Teji says 876 and 321 too have the same beat!
Now, numbers that repeat, just the same,
11, 22, 33, have twin digits.
111, 222, 333 are triplet digits.
Can you find more such numbers that follow the pattern?
Here are more numbers that look the same,
from left to right, and right to left:
353, 868.
Finding them is a fun game. Write other such numbers.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Numbers with Slips
content.concept: Forming numbers using paper slips.
content.explanation: You have 6 slips where you can write 100, 10, or 1. What numbers can you make? Let's find out! ✏️

practice_questions.q: What will you write on these slips for making 231?
practice_questions.a: 2 slips of 100, 3 slips of 10, 1 slip of 1

practice_questions.q: What will you write on these slips for making 123?
practice_questions.a: 1 slip of 100, 2 slips of 10, 3 slips of 1

content.subject: maths
content.title: Making Numbers with Digits
content.concept: Creating numbers using specific digits.
content.explanation: Using the digits 3 and 8, let's make as many 2 or 3 digit numbers as we can! 🌟

practice_questions.q: What is the smallest number you can make?
What is the largest number you can make?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Word Numerals
content.concept: Learning to express numbers using words.
content.explanation: Bhutasankhya means using words to say numbers! For example, instead of saying '1', we can say 'one'. Let's practice! 🎉

practice_questions.q: What is the word for the number 5?
How do you say 7 in words?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Measuring Length
content.concept: Understanding different ways to measure length.
content.explanation: We can measure things using our hands, feet, or even strings! 🖐️👣 Let's explore how we can measure lengths in fun ways!

practice_questions.q: How do Leena and Adi measure the table?
What can you use to measure the height of a door?
practice_questions.a: You can use a string or your hand!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Decorate the Board
content.concept: Using colorful strings to decorate and measure.
content.explanation: Let's have fun decorating! 🎨 Use strings of different colors to make your board look beautiful! How many strings do you need?

practice_questions.q: How many more colorful paper strings will be needed?
How many strings are needed to decorate the entire border?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Shelly and Adi's Challenge
content.concept: Finding ways to measure and fit objects.
content.explanation: Shelly and Adi have a heavy table, and they need to figure out if it can go through the door without lifting it! Let's help them! 🏠

practice_questions.q: How can they check if the table fits through the door?
practice_questions.a: By measuring the table with a string!

practice_questions.q: What if the table is too big?
practice_questions.a: They can try turning it or measuring differently!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Measurement
content.concept: Measuring objects using different tools.
content.explanation: We can measure things using different tools like pencils, paper strips, and ropes. 📏 Let's see how we can measure the length of a saree using a paper strip! If a pencil is too short, we can use a longer paper strip! 📜
examples: Measuring a saree with a paper strip
Using a metre rod to measure a rope

practice_questions.q: Is measuring with a pencil faster than with a paper strip?
practice_questions.a: No, a paper strip can be longer and faster!

practice_questions.q: Can we measure the length of our height using a rope?
practice_questions.a: Yes, we can use a rope to measure our height!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Measure Your Height
content.concept: Comparing heights of friends.
content.explanation: Let's measure our heights! 📏 Mark one metre on the wall and see who is taller. Write down the names of your friends who are taller or shorter than one metre! 🌟

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding Object Lengths
content.concept: Identifying lengths of various objects.
content.explanation: Look around you! Can you find objects that are one metre long, more than one metre, or less than one metre? 🧸📏 Let's write them down!
examples: A door is more than one metre
A pencil is less than one metre

content.subject: maths
content.title: Jumping Measurement
content.concept: Measuring how far we can jump.
content.explanation: Let's see how far we can jump! 🏃‍♂️ Mark a start line and jump! Measure how far you jumped using a rope. Was it more than a quarter metre? Let's find out! 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Estimating Lengths
content.concept: Estimating and verifying lengths.
content.explanation: Can you estimate how long one metre is? 🤔 Cut a piece of wool or thread and see if it's really one metre! Let's check with our friends! 📏

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Metres
content.concept: Measuring length using a metre rope.
content.explanation: A metre rope helps us measure things! We can use it to see how long something is, like a wall. 🏠
examples: A rope that is 1 metre long can be used to measure the length of your classroom wall.

practice_questions.q: How many cuts did you make if you cut a one-metre long rope into 4 equal pieces?
How many footsteps fit into a metre rope?
practice_questions.a: Depends on the length of your foot!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Filling and Lifting
content.concept: Understanding the capacity of different vessels.
content.explanation: Different containers hold different amounts of liquid. Let's find out which one holds more! 🥛
examples: A glass holds less than a jug, but more than a small bowl.

practice_questions.q: Who drank the most milk?
practice_questions.a: You need to compare the glasses!

practice_questions.q: Fill in the blanks with ‘more’ or ‘less’: Nita’s glass holds ............. milk than Monu’s glass.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guess and Check
content.concept: Estimating and measuring capacity.
content.explanation: Let's guess how many small bowls will fill a glass and then check if we are right! 🎉

practice_questions.q: How many ladles fill the bowl?
How many glasses can be filled by the jug?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Whose Glass Holds More?
content.concept: Comparing the capacity of different glasses.
content.explanation: Let's find out whose glass holds the most milk! 🥛
examples: Ritu’s glass, Monu’s glass, and Nita’s glass.

practice_questions.q: Who drank the least milk?
practice_questions.a: Check the size of the glasses!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Nita and the Milkman
content.concept: Understanding why measuring cups are used.
content.explanation: Nita sees the milkman pouring milk using a measuring cup every day. Why do you think he does that? 🤔

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding 1 Litre
content.concept: Identifying vessels that hold 1 litre or more.
content.explanation: Let's explore different vessels to see if they can hold 1 litre or not! 🥛 Use a 1 litre bottle to check if your jug, glass, bowl, or ladle can hold the same amount.
examples: A jug holds more than 1 litre.
A glass holds less than 1 litre.

practice_questions.q: Does the mug hold a litre or half litre of water?
Does the glass hold a litre, half litre, or quarter litre of water?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Heavy or Light?
content.concept: Understanding the concept of weight through comparison.
content.explanation: Let's compare different objects to see which one is heavier or lighter! 🏋️‍♂️ Use your lunch box and pencil box to find out!
examples: Lunch box is heavier than pencil box.
A balloon filled with air is lighter than a basketball.

practice_questions.q: Which is heavier: your school bag or lunch bag?
Is an apple heavier or lighter than a watermelon?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guess the Weight
content.concept: Estimating weights using matchboxes.
content.explanation: Fill a matchbox with sand and use it to guess the weight of different objects! 🏋️‍♀️
examples: Guess how many matchboxes it takes to balance a pencil box.

practice_questions.q: How many matchboxes do you think it will take to balance a spoon?
How many matchboxes are needed to balance 4 marbles?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Find Your Bag's Weight
content.concept: Comparing weights of bags in groups.
content.explanation: In groups, find a bag that is heavier and one that is lighter than yours! 👜 Discuss why they are different.
examples: Count the number of books in each bag.

practice_questions.q: Why is your bag heavier or lighter?
practice_questions.a: Because of the number of books.

practice_questions.q: Can you make the two bags of about the same weight by moving a book?

content.subject: maths
content.title: What is 1 Kilogram?
content.concept: Identifying objects that weigh 1 kilogram.
content.explanation: With your parents, find objects at home that weigh 1 kilogram! 🏠 Feel them and guess what else might weigh the same.
examples: A bag of sugar weighs 1 kilogram.

practice_questions.q: What other objects do you think weigh 1 kilogram?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Kilograms
content.concept: Identifying objects that weigh 1 kilogram.
content.explanation: Let's learn about weight! We can use a 1 kg salt packet to help us measure. Can you think of objects that might weigh 1 kg? 🤔
examples: A packet of sugar weighs 1 kg 🍬
A small bag of rice weighs 1 kg 🍚

practice_questions.q: Write the names of the objects that are 1 kilogram.
practice_questions.a: Examples include a packet of salt or sugar.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guessing Weights
content.concept: Comparing weights of objects.
content.explanation: Can you guess which objects are heavier or lighter than 1 kg? Use a tick mark to show your guesses! ✔️

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using a Pan-Balance
content.concept: Identifying heavier and lighter balls using a pan-balance.
content.explanation: Montu has a puzzle with 3 balls! One is heavier, and two are the same weight. Can you find the heavy ball using the pan-balance? ⚖️
examples: Red ball, Orange ball, Green ball

practice_questions.q: How many times will you have to weigh?
practice_questions.a: You can find the heavy ball in just one weighing!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Kishan and His Saplings
content.concept: Adding and subtracting saplings.
content.explanation: Kishan has 364 saplings and he gets 52 more. How many saplings does he have now? Let's add them together! 🌱

practice_questions.q: How many saplings does Kishan have now?

content.subject: maths
content.title: How Many More Saplings?
content.concept: Subtracting saplings to find out how many more are needed.
content.explanation: Kishan needs to deliver 230 saplings but has packed 75. How many more does he need to pack? Let's subtract! 📦

practice_questions.q: How many more saplings does Kishan need to pack?
practice_questions.a: He needs to pack 155 more saplings!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Blocks of 10s
content.concept: Understanding hundreds, tens, and ones.
content.explanation: Numbers can be broken down into hundreds, tens, and ones. For example, the number 225 has 2 hundreds, 2 tens, and 5 ones. Let's visualize it! 🎉

content.subject: maths
content.title: Kishan's Saplings
content.concept: Solving word problems using subtraction and addition.
content.explanation: Let's help Kishan figure out how many saplings he has left after giving some away! 🌱

practice_questions.q: Kishan has 456 saplings and gives away 63. How many does he have left?
Kishan has 309 saplings and gets 80 more. How many does he have now?
Kishan has 270 saplings and his friend has 36. How many more does Kishan have?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using the Grid
content.concept: Solving addition problems using a grid.
content.explanation: Use the grid to find answers to the addition problems. Color your answers for fun! 🎨

content.subject: maths
content.title: The Village of Jadupur
content.concept: Understanding barter system and exchange.
content.explanation: In the village of Jadupur, people exchanged goods based on their needs. Let's see how they did it! 🏡
content.narrative: Many years ago, in the Village ‘Jadupur’, people exchanged things based on their need. Shaamu Kaka gave 5 sacks of rice to Dariya Didi. She in return gave 10 sacks of vegetables. Dariya Didi got 2 sarees from Bablu Dada by giving 5 sacks of onions. Like this, people in the village exchanged their things. Discuss in class why people in this village had to give different quantities while exchanging things.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Using Notes and Coins
content.concept: Understanding how to use money for purchases.
content.explanation: Let's learn how to use different notes and coins to buy things! 💰

practice_questions.q: How many ₹100 notes are equal to a ₹500 note?
What things can you buy with ₹100?
practice_questions.a: Two bottles of milk or a basket of pomegranates.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Earnings
content.concept: Calculating earnings from money transactions.
content.explanation: When you have some money and earn more, you can find out how much you have earned by subtracting the original amount from the new amount. For example, if you start with ₹465 and end with ₹756, you earned ₹291! 🎉
examples: If you have ₹100 and earn ₹50, you now have ₹150!

practice_questions.q: In the morning, Peter uncle has ₹465. By afternoon, he has ₹756. How much has he earned since morning?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Buying and Returning Money
content.concept: Calculating change after a purchase.
content.explanation: When you buy something and give more money than the cost, you get change back! If Reena buys groceries for ₹209 and pays with a ₹500 note, she should get back ₹291! 💵
examples: If you buy a toy for ₹150 and pay with ₹200, your change is ₹50!

practice_questions.q: Reena bought groceries for ₹209. She gave a ₹500 note to Peter uncle. How much money should Peter uncle return to Reena?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Saving Over Time
content.concept: Calculating total savings over several weeks.
content.explanation: If you save some money every week, you can find out how much you have saved in total by adding up your savings. For example, if Shireen saves ₹100 every week for 4 weeks, she will have ₹400! 💰
examples: If you save ₹20 each week for 5 weeks, you will have ₹100!

practice_questions.q: Shireen has ₹150 in her piggy bank. She puts ₹100 every week in her piggy bank. How much money does she have at the end of four weeks?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Calculating Total Savings
content.concept: Finding total savings over multiple months.
content.explanation: To find out how much money you have saved over several months, just add up all the amounts saved each month! If Peter uncle saved ₹250 in the first month, ₹125 in the second month, and ₹350 in the third month, he saved a total of ₹725! 📊
examples: If you save ₹100 in January, ₹150 in February, and ₹200 in March, your total savings is ₹450!

practice_questions.q: Peter uncle saved ₹250 in the first month, ₹125 in the second month, and ₹350 in the third month. How much has he saved in these three months?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Estimating Numbers
content.concept: Rounding numbers to the nearest hundred.
content.explanation: When you want to estimate a number, you can round it to the nearest hundred. For example, 156 rounds to 200 and 34 rounds to 0. This makes it easier to add or subtract! 🔢

practice_questions.q: Estimate the nearest hundred for 156 + 34
Estimate the nearest hundred for 125 - 15
Estimate the nearest hundred for 105 + 195

content.subject: maths
content.title: Time Goes On
content.concept: Understanding the calendar months.
content.explanation: This fun rhyme helps us remember the months of the year and how they can sometimes get mixed up! 🗓️
content.text:
Appa bought a new calendar,
Amma points a big blunder.
Missing was the month of July,
I wonder, where did it fly?
Don’t worry, don’t worry,
I have a solution to your query.
July and January looks the same,
Let us play the calendar game.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding the School Reopening Date
content.concept: Calculating the date when school reopens after closure.
content.explanation: If the school closes on July 7 for 15 days, we can find out when it will reopen by adding 15 days to July 7. Let's count together! 📅

practice_questions.q: What date will the school reopen?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Making a Calendar for July 2024
content.concept: Creating and understanding a monthly calendar.
content.explanation: Let's make a fun calendar for July 2024! 🌞 We will write down the days and dates. Can you help fill it in?
examples: July 1 is a Monday, July 2 is a Tuesday, and so on.

practice_questions.q: What day is July 4, 2024?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Calendar Observations
content.concept: Identifying similarities and differences in calendars.
content.explanation: Look at the calendars from the last two years. What do you see that is the same? What has changed? Let's tick the right answers! ✔️
examples: Months names are the same, but the days can change.

practice_questions.q: Do the names of the months change from year to year?
How many Sundays are there in a year?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Guessing Ages
content.concept: Understanding age relationships.
content.explanation: Hetal is twice as old as her brother and 10 years older. Can you guess their ages? Let's think together! 🤔
examples: If Hetal is 10, her brother is 5.

practice_questions.q: If Hetal is 12, how old is her brother?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Age Fun Activity
content.concept: Calculating ages based on family relationships.
content.explanation: Let's find out how old everyone is! Fill in the blanks with your family's ages. 🧑‍👩‍👧
examples: My age today: ___, Age of my mother’s mother when my mother was born: ___.

practice_questions.q: How old is your mother today?
How old was your mother when you were born?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Reading Birth Certificates
content.concept: Understanding birth certificates and calculating age.
content.explanation: Look at Bincy's birth certificate and answer the questions! 📜

practice_questions.q: What date was Bincy born?
How old will Bincy be on 2 May 2025?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Your Own Birth Certificate
content.concept: Creating a personal birth certificate.
content.explanation: Let's create your own birth certificate! Fill in the details. 🖊️

practice_questions.q: What is your name?
What is your date of birth?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Observing Clocks
content.concept: Understanding how to read and observe clocks.
content.explanation: Get a working clock and observe how the hands move. Can you tell the time? ⏰
examples: The short hand shows the hour, the long hand shows the minutes.

practice_questions.q: What does the short hand on the clock show?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Time
content.concept: Learning to read time on a clock.
content.explanation: A clock has two hands: the hour hand and the minute hand. The hour hand shows the hour, and the minute hand shows the minutes. 🕒 Let's learn how to tell time!

practice_questions.q: How many minutes did Hetal take to eat her breakfast?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Draw the Clock Hands
content.concept: Practicing drawing hour and minute hands.
content.explanation: Let's draw the hour and minute hands on a clock for different times! 🕑

content.subject: maths
content.title: Match Activities with Time
content.concept: Connecting daily activities with the time they take.
content.explanation: Match the activity with the time shown on the clock! 🕘

content.subject: maths
content.title: Fill the Time Table
content.concept: Understanding different durations of time.
content.explanation: Fill in the table with activities that take different amounts of time! ⏳

content.subject: maths
content.title: Calculate Time Taken
content.concept: Calculating time taken for activities.
content.explanation: Look at the clock and calculate how much time has passed! ⏰

content.subject: maths
content.title: Analog vs Digital Clocks
content.concept: Understanding the difference between analog and digital clocks.
content.explanation: An analog clock shows time with hands, while a digital clock shows time with numbers. 🕰️

practice_questions.q: What is the difference between an analog clock and a digital clock?
practice_questions.a: Analog clocks have hands, digital clocks show numbers.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Make Your Own Sand Clock
content.concept: Creating a timer using sand.
content.explanation: Let's make a sand clock to measure time! ⏳ Follow the steps carefully.

content.subject: maths
content.topic: A Day in the Life of Hetal
content.title: Hetal's Daily Routine
content.concept: Understanding daily activities and their timings.
content.explanation: Join Hetal as she goes through her day, learning about time! 🌞

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Symmetry
content.concept: Identifying symmetrical and non-symmetrical objects.
content.explanation: Symmetry means that one half of an object is a mirror image of the other half. For example, if you fold a butterfly in half, both sides look the same! 🦋

practice_questions.q: How many malas can be made?
practice_questions.a: You can make different malas by using various colors and arrangements.

practice_questions.q: Tick the malas that are symmetrical.
practice_questions.a: The malas with two identical halves are symmetrical.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Create Your Own Mala
content.concept: Using beads to create symmetrical malas.
content.explanation: Let's make malas with beads! Use 4 beads of one color and 4 of another to create a symmetrical pattern. 🌈
examples: 4 red beads and 4 blue beads make a symmetrical mala.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Exploring Rangolis
content.concept: Understanding symmetrical rangolis.
content.explanation: Rangolis are beautiful patterns made on the ground. Some are symmetrical, meaning they look the same on both sides! 🎨
examples: A rangoli with 6 dots on each side is symmetrical.

practice_questions.q: Are all rangolis symmetrical?
practice_questions.a: No, some rangolis can be asymmetrical.

practice_questions.q: Draw and complete the symmetrical rangolis given below.
practice_questions.a: Use your creativity to finish the patterns!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Create Your Own Rangoli
content.concept: Drawing symmetrical rangolis.
content.explanation: Draw some more rangolis in your notebook that are symmetrical! Use dots to help you. 🌼

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Symmetrical Masks
content.concept: Understanding how to make symmetrical masks.
content.explanation: Let's make a mask! Fold a paper, draw on one side, and cut it out. When you open it, you’ll have a symmetrical mask! 🎭
examples: A cat mask drawn and cut out symmetrically.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Mirror Images
content.concept: Mirror images are reflections of objects.
content.explanation: When you look at something in a mirror, you see its reflection. Just like how Soni and Avi played with counters, the counters on one side are the mirror image of the other side! 🪞✨
examples: 🟡🟡🟡🟡 on one side and 🟡🟡🟡🟡 on the other side

practice_questions.q: What is the trick the painter is playing?
practice_questions.a: The painter is drawing only half of the picture.

practice_questions.q: Can you place counters to create a mirror image?
practice_questions.a: Yes, by placing them opposite to each other.

content.subject: maths
content.title: Playing the Mirror Game
content.concept: Using mirrors to understand symmetry.
content.explanation: In the mirror game, you can see how objects reflect and create symmetry. Let's play with counters! 🎲
examples: Place 4 counters on one side and see the mirror image on the other side.

practice_questions.q: Has Avi placed the counters at the right places?
Can you rearrange Micy's side to match Catty's?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Creating Tiled Paths
content.concept: Using shapes to fill spaces without gaps.
content.explanation: When we create paths with tiles, we need to make sure there are no gaps or overlaps. Let's use rangometry shapes! 🟩🟦
examples: Fill a square with triangle tiles without leaving any gaps.

practice_questions.q: How can you fill shapes with no gaps?
What happens if there are gaps between tiles?
practice_questions.a: It won't look nice and won't be complete!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding Dada and Dadi
content.concept: Using directions to find someone.
content.explanation: Soni and Avi are looking for their Dada and Dadi at the fair. They need to follow directions to find them! 🧭
examples: Take 2 steps to your right, then 1 step forward.

practice_questions.q: Where is the chaupal?
practice_questions.a: It's where Dada and Dadi are waiting.

practice_questions.q: Can you help find the chaupal?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding the Map of the Fair
content.concept: Learning to read and interpret a map.
content.explanation: Maps help us find our way! Just like a treasure map, it shows us where to go! 🗺️✨
examples: The hut shows the handicraft stalls. 🏠
The play area is marked with a fun picture! 🎡

practice_questions.q: Which place does the sign show?
practice_questions.a: Look at the map to find the answer!

practice_questions.q: Circle the picture in the map that shows the play area?
practice_questions.a: Find the picture of the play area and circle it!

practice_questions.q: How many exit routes are there in the fair?
practice_questions.a: Count the exit signs on the map!

content.subject: maths
content.title: Navigating the Fair
content.concept: Learning to follow directions step by step.
content.explanation: Following directions is like a fun game! 🎮 Let's go on an adventure! 🏃‍♂️🏃‍♀️
examples: Turn right on the green lane. ➕

practice_questions.q: What do you see when you turn right?
Where do you meet Dada Dadi?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Finding the Way Out
content.concept: Exploring paths and sharing experiences.
content.explanation: Let's find our way out of the maze together! 🌀 What did you see on the way? 🌟

practice_questions.q: Share the way you went through the maze. What did you find?

content.subject: maths
content.title: Understanding Numbers
content.concept: Learning to recognize and use numbers.
content.explanation: Numbers are everywhere! Let's learn to read them! 🔢✨

practice_questions.q: What number comes after 5?
Can you count to 10?

content.subject: maths
